English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Henry N. Ricciuti papers
Ricciuti, Henry N.
National Institute of Child Health and Human Development (U.S.)
23-13-2551
8.5 cubic feet. 8.5 cubic feet.
1946-2008. Files. Files relating to the career of Henry N. Ricciuti, Professor of Human Development and Family Studies in the New York State College of Human Ecology. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 6 7 ======= 6 7 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2100932
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
M.E. Warren
Date completed:
November 1990
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, March 2004
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, July 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of Human Development and Family Studies, New York State College of Human Ecology.
Henry Ricciuti earned his B.S., M.A. and Ph.D. (1949), all in psychology from Fordham University, and served in the U.S. Army from 1943 to 1946. Prior to joining the Cornell faculty in 1958, he served as an assistant professor at the University of Connecticut, research associate at the Educational Testing Service, and assistant professor at the University of Colorado Medical School. At Cornell, he was a professor in the Dept. of Human Development and Family Studies (1958-1988), specialized in early childhood development, especially the factors associated with cognitive and emotional development of infants and young children. He was an active emeritus professor from 1988-2011.
SERIES LIST
Series I. College of Home Economics Organization Committee
Series II. Faculty Committee on Student Affairs (FCSA)
Series III. Infant Day Care and Resource Center
Series IV. Cornell-Tompkins Day Care Project
Series V. University Related Files
Series VI. Department Related Files
Series VII. International Programs
Series VIII. College Related Files
Series IX. Proposals, Studies, Research
Series X. Photographs
Series XI. Publications
Series XII. Correspondence
Series XIII. Subject Files
Series XIV. National Institute of Child Health and Human Development (NICHD)
Cite As:
Henry N. Ricciuti papers, #23-13-2551. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Files relating to the reorganization of the College of Human Ecology, particularly the Reorganization Committee, chaired by Dr. Ricciuti; files relating to his membership on the Faculty Committee on Student Affairs, 1966-1969, with much material on events leading up to the Straight takeover in April 1969; and materials relating to Cornell's involvement with day care, starting with the Cornell Day Care Committee, 1971-1972; the Cornell-Tompkins County Day Care Project, 1972-1974; the Infant Care and Resource Center, operated in Sage Infirmary, 1975-1980, under a grant from the Appalachian Regional Commission; and the unsuccessful efforts to continue to offer work-site infant day care on campus.
Scope and content
Includes material relating to the National Institute of Child Health and Human Developmpent (NICHD).
Photographs. Students -- Political activity. African American student movements -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Day care centers -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Employer-supported day care. New York State College of Human Ecology Cornell University -- : Students. Cornell University -- : Faculty. Cornell University. Faculty Committee on Student Affairs Cornell University.
